title: FOSTER GRANDPARENT PROGRAM - 211info
text: COMMUNITY VOLUNTEER NETWORK FOSTER GRANDPARENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a volunteer opportunity for older adults to connect with children and youth. Volunteers receive a stipend for each hour of service as well as transportation reimbursement, paid time off, training, accident, personal liability, and excess automobile liability insurance as well as community recognition. The stipend is tax-free and is non-declarable and will not affect any other State or Federal benefits of participants. 1 West Main Street Suite 303 Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: To volunteer: -Age 55 and older and in good health -Ability to pass State and FBI background checks -Household income at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: No standard hours, volunteer service hours 5-40 hours per week Email (541) 857-7786 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 857-7786 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Jackson, Josephine, Klamath Counties Services: Senior Corps Volunteer Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: EMPLOYMENT - RSVP HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - SENIOR HEALTH INSURANCE BENEFITS ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. COMMUNITY VOLUNTEER NETWORK
